revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24755395338|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami-jinja (Kamisato)]][[ja:今城青坂稲実池上神社 (上里町忍保)]] {{Expand Japanese|今城青坂稲実池上神社 (上里町忍保)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami Shrine | native_name = 今城青坂稲実池上神社 | image = ImakiAosakaInamiIkegami-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Shibo).jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.269083333333334|139.1538888888889}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami Shrine'''|今城青坂稲実池上神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 37 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039747|ltq=Q135039747|qid=Q135039747|1=Imakiawosakanoinamiaramitamano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q2963366|ltq=Q2963366|qid=Q2963366|1=Chichibu Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Kamisato, Saitama|lt=Kamisato|arz|كاميساتو|ceb|Kamisato Machi|de|Kamisato (Saitama)|en|Kamisato, Saitama|es|Kamisato|fa|کامیساتو، سایتاما|fr|Kamisato|id|Kamisato, Saitama|it|Kamisato|ja|上里町|javoyage|上里町|ko|가미사토정|ms|Kamisato, Saitama|my|ခမိဆတိုမြို့၊ ဆိုင်းတမ|pl|Kamisato|ru|Камисато|sv|Kamisato|tl|Kamisato, Saitama|tr|Kamisato|uk|Камісато (Сайтама)|zh|上里町|qid=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}} * {{ill|Q907398|ltq=Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} * {{ill|Q11634927|ltq=Q11634927|qid=Q11634927|1=Kami district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.269083333333334|139.1538888888889}} * {{coord|36.269184|139.153937}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * ImakiAosakaInamiIkegami-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Shibo).jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181725 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いまきあおやさかいなみいけがみじんじゃ * -イケノウヘノEngishiki Jinmyōchō == Commons category (P373) == * Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami-jinja (Kamisato) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039748|ltq=Q135039748|qid=Q135039748|1=Imakiawosakainaminoikekamino Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県児玉郡上里町忍保225', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami-jinja (Kamisato)}} [[JA:今城青坂稲実池上神社 (上里町忍保)]] {{wikidata link|Q72727923}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Imaki Aosaka Inami Ikegami Shrine |Image = [[File:ImakiAosakaInamiIkegami-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Shibo).jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kodama District|Q938038|qid=Q938038|1=Kodama District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Kamisato Town|Q1345287|qid=Q1345287|1=Kamisato, Saitama}} Shibo 225 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|qid=Q11588504|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|1=Haraedo no Kami}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Unknown |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = Ikegami Shrine |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = Sunday closest to October 17 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Imaki Aosaka Inami Ikegami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Imaki Aosaka Inami Ikegami Shrine''' (いまきあおさかいなみいけがみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Shibo, Kamisato Town, Kodama District, Saitama Prefecture, along the {{ill|lt=Karasu River (Tone River system)|Q3915532|qid=Q3915532|lt=Karasu River|1=Karasu River (Gunma)}}, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Tone River|Q177452|qid=Q177452|1=Tone River}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|qid=Q11588504|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Mikoto|1=Haraedo no Kami}} and {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|1=Toyouke-hime}}. {{Citation needed range|It is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) of the Engishiki-listed “Imaki Aoyasaka Inami Ikegami Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}{{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|qid=Q11634927|1=Kami district}}, and its former shrine rank was Prefectural Shrine|date=February 2025}}. === History === A clan of immigrant origin with advanced rice-cultivation technology enshrined the rice deity “Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto” as their tutelary deity and founded the shrine in Wadō 4 (711) in Shibo along the Karasu River. Shrine noticeboard, “The 62nd Ise Grand Shrine Shikinen Sengū Commemoration: History of Imaki Aoyasaka Inami Ikegami Shrine” Later, as the immigrant clan declined, the shrine fell into ruin, {{Citation needed range|the shrine name “Imaki Aoyasaka Inami Ikegami Shrine” was lost|date=February 2025}} and it came to be called “Inari Shrine.” During the {{ill|lt=Genkō|Q1194556|qid=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} era (1331–1334), {{ill|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|Q1328796|qid=Q1328796|1=Nitta Yoshisada}} rebuilt it as protection against the northeast (kimon) for Kanakubo Castle. At this time, for apotropaic purposes, the Haraedo deity “{{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|qid=Q11588504|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Mikoto|1=Haraedo no Kami}}” is thought to have been enshrined. In {{ill|lt=Engen|Q1194159|qid=Q1194159|1=Engen}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1338|Q6044|qid=Q6044|1=1338}}), Tokiko Yoshimune became lord of Kanakubo and prayed for victory in battle. In {{ill|lt=Jōji|Q1146153|qid=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1363|Q6176|qid=Q6176|1=1363}}), when {{ill|lt=Uesugi Noriaki|Q11359188|qid=Q11359188|1=Uesugi Noriaki}} became {{ill|lt=Kantō Kanrei|Q5367751|qid=Q5367751|1=Kantō Kanrei}} and lord of Kanakubo, he prayed for peace along the borders in {{ill|lt=Ōan|Q1146245|qid=Q1146245|1=Ōan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1368|Q6207|qid=Q6207|1=1368}}). In {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|qid=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1524|Q6309|qid=Q6309|1=1524}}), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saitō Morimitsu|1=UNKNOWN}} became lord of Kanakubo Castle and, like the Nitta clan, made the shrine the castle’s guardian deity. Shrine noticeboard “Ikegami Shrine” (installed by Kamisato Town, Saitama Prefecture; March 1986) In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1582|Q6719|qid=Q6719|1=1582}}) June, {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q11634927|qid=Q11634927|1=Kami district}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} became a battlefield in the “{{ill|lt=Battle of the Kanna River|Q1058786|qid=Q1058786|1=Battle of Kanagawa}}” between {{ill|lt=Takigawa Kazumasu|Q953624|qid=Q953624|1=Takigawa Kazumasu}}, a retainer of {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the Hōjō forces led by {{ill|lt=Hōjō Ujimasa|Q736948|qid=Q736948|1=Hōjō Ujimasa}}. The shrine buildings were destroyed by fire. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1590|Q6764|qid=Q6764|1=1590}}) January, Kawakubo Nobutoshi became lord of Kanakubo, and in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|qid=Q6766|1=1591}}) he rebuilt the shrine buildings that had burned down and donated sacred rice fields. Kawakubo Nobutoshi was the son of “{{ill|lt=Takeda Nobuzane|Q11546097|qid=Q11546097|1=Takeda Nobuzane}},” younger brother of {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|qid=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}}. In {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1694|Q7694|qid=Q7694|1=1694}}), flooding of the Karasu River swept away the shrine buildings. The washed-away main hall was recovered, repaired, and rebuilt on a raised foundation. In {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1698|Q7706|qid=Q7706|1=1698}}), {{ill|lt=Takeda Nobusada|Q56353014|qid=Q56353014|1=Takeda Nobusada}}, grandson of Kawakubo Nobutoshi, was transferred to {{ill|lt=Tango Province|Q1054331|qid=Q1054331|1=Tango Province}}. Kanakubo Castle was abandoned, and the shrine declined. In {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1738|Q6996|qid=Q6996|1=1738}}), the local land steward rebuilt the torii gate and offered rice. During the {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|qid=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} era ({{ill|lt=1848|Q7647|qid=Q7647|1=1848}}–{{ill|lt=1854|Q7670|qid=Q7670|1=1854}}), the land steward repaired the shrine buildings and donated sacred rice fields. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), the shrine name was restored from “Inari Shrine” to the current “Imaki Aosaka Inami Ikegami Shrine.” '''Shrine rank''' * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}) – Village Shrine * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 32 ({{ill|lt=1899|Q7851|qid=Q7851|1=1899}}) February 7 – Prefectural Shrine * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|qid=Q2048|1=1907}}) January 12 – Shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and paper streamers File:ImakiAosakaInamiIkegami-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Shibo) Torii1.jpg|First torii File:ImakiAosakaInamiIkegami-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Shibo) Torii2.jpg|Second torii File:ImakiAosakaInamiIkegami-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Shibo) Kaguraden1.jpg|Kagura hall === Enshrined deities === [[File:Imakiaoyasakainamiikegami-jinja(Kamisato) Hengaku1.jpg|150px|thumb|{{center|Plaque at the first torii}}]] [[File:Imakiaoyasakainamiikegami-jinja(Kamisato) Hengaku2.jpg|150px|thumb|{{center|Plaque at the worship hall}}]] * {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|qid=Q11588504|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Mikoto|1=Haraedo no Kami}} (いぶきどぬしのみこと) '''Enshrined together''' * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|1=Toyouke-hime}} (とようけひめのかみ) === Auxiliary shrines Heisei Festival Data (publisher: Association of Shinto Shrines; June 1995) === * Yasaka Shrine (Enshrined deity) {{ill|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Q272993|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Raiden Shrine (Enshrined deity) {{ill|lt=Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534|qid=Q10892534|lt=Eight Thunder Deities|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} * Miyanishi Shrine (Enshrined deity) {{ill|lt=Yakuso-no-Kami|Q11622504|qid=Q11622504|1=Three Medicine Gods}} File:ImakiAosakaInamiIkegami-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Shibo) Yasaka-jinja.jpg|Yasaka Shrine File:ImakiAosakaInamiIkegami-jinja(Kamisato-machi,Shibo) Miyanishi-jinja 2.jpg|Miyanishi Shrine === Events === * New Year’s Day Festival (January 2) * Prayer for a Good Harvest (March 17) * Great Purification Rite (June 30) * Sacred Rice Field Festival (July 25) * Grand Annual Festival (Sunday closest to October 17) * Harvest Festival (Sunday closest to November 27) * Great Purification Rite (December 29) === Access === * From JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|qid=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}} {{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|qid=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}}, take the Kamisato Town bus {{ill|lt=Komugicchi-go|Q28682946|qid=Q28682946|1=Komugicchi-go}} (Northern Route) bound for “Unix.” Get off at “Ikegami Shrine.” * 25-minute walk (1.9 km) from JR {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|qid=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}} {{ill|lt=Jinbohara Station|Q845133|qid=Q845133|1=Jimbohara Station}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Imaki-Aosaka-Inami-Ikegami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:上里町の建築物]] [[Category:上里町の歴史]]
